title: Understanding Our Changing Climate
passage:
Our Warming Earth

The Earth is our home, and it gives us everything we need. But today, our planet is getting warmer. This warming is called global warming, and it causes climate change. Scientists say the temperature of the Earth is rising every year. This is a big problem for people, plants, and animals.

Why is the Earth getting warmer? It is mostly because of human activities. We use cars, buses, and planes every day. These vehicles burn oil and gas, which make the air dirty. Factories also put bad gases into the air. These gases are called greenhouse gases. They trap heat from the sun, just like a glass greenhouse keeps plants warm. Also, people cut down many trees to build houses and roads. Trees are important because they clean our air. Without trees, the air gets warmer quickly.

Global warming changes our weather and environment in dangerous ways. In cold places like the Arctic, the ice is melting very fast. Because the ice melts, the water in the oceans is rising. This can cause floods in cities near the sea. Many animals are also in danger. For example, polar bears live on the sea ice. When the ice melts, they lose their homes and cannot find enough food to eat.

We can all do simple things to help our planet. First, we can walk, ride bicycles, or use buses instead of driving private cars. This keeps the air clean. Second, we can turn off the lights, computers, and televisions when we do not use them. This saves a lot of electricity. Finally, we can plant more trees in our gardens and schools. Every small action can help make our Earth a beautiful and safe place to live.

questions.text: What is the main topic of the text?
questions.explanation: The text is about global warming and climate change, which means the warming of the Earth.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Trees are important because they clean our air.'

questions.text: What happens in the Arctic because of global warming?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'In cold places like the Arctic, the ice is melting very fast.'

vocabulary.meaning: how hot or cold something is
gases in the air that trap heat from the sun
changing from solid ice into liquid water because of heat
large amounts of water covering an area that is usually dry
power/energy used to make lights and machines work

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ A1-A2, câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ gần như giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy khoanh vùng từ khóa (ví dụ: 'temperature', 'Arctic', 'electricity') để tìm đoạn văn chứa câu trả lời nhanh chóng.

2. True/False/Not Given: Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi hoàn toàn không được nhắc đến trong bài (ví dụ: chi phí trồng cây 'expensive'), hãy chọn ngay NOT GIVEN.

3. Giới hạn từ: Chú ý yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS'. Chỉ điền tối đa 2 từ lấy chính xác từ bài đọc, không tự ý thay đổi dạng của từ.
